chunks.page_summary: This document outlines a procedure for verifying Modbus TCP communication with Rheonics viscosity and density sensors using the ModScan software.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
Quick Modbus TCP verification using ModScan
Modified on: Thu, 9 Jan, 2025 at 10:06 PM

What products are involved?
Rheonics SRV viscometer (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/), SRD density-viscosity meter (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/), DVP gas density-viscosity meter (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-gas-lng-density-meter-dvp/) and DVM HPHT density-viscosity meter (https://rheonics.com/products/hpht-pvt-density-meter-viscometer-dvm/).

What is the purpose of this article?
This article describes the procedure for reading Modbus Input Register from Rheonics Viscosity and Density sensors using ModScan

1. Overview
ModScan is a Windows application that operates as a Modbus master. It is ideal for quick and easy testing of Rheonics Modbus TCP protocol (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000217062) by validating communication parameters, such as register types and data formats, before full integration into the process or for troubleshooting purposes. ModScan can be downloaded from WinTECH's website (https://www.win-tech.com/).

This article will provide step-by-step instructions on how to use ModScan to successfully read Modbus TCP Input Registers from Rheonics viscosity and density sensors.

2. Intro to ModScan
The first step is to connect to Rheonics Sensor via TCP/IP from the PC. Usually this is done by connecting an Ethernet (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000219061) Cable to the Rheonics SME Ethernet port.
On the PC, open ModScan64 software and click on Connection menu, then on Connect.
key_facts: The document provides a procedure for reading Modbus Input Registers from Rheonics Viscosity and Density sensors using ModScan.
ModScan is a Windows application functioning as a Modbus master, used for quick and easy testing of Rheonics Modbus TCP protocol.
ModScan helps validate communication parameters like register types and data formats for integration or troubleshooting.
ModScan can be downloaded from WinTECH's website.
The initial step involves connecting the Rheonics Sensor to a PC via TCP/IP, typically using an Ethernet cable to the Rheonics SME Ethernet port.
To connect, users must open ModScan64 software on the PC and navigate to the 'Connection' menu, then click 'Connect'.

key_facts: The ModScan software application is shown in a 'Not Connected' state, displaying Modbus coil status.
The ModScan software allows configuration of Modbus communication parameters, including device address, ID, length, and point type (e.g., COIL STATUS).
Connection details for Modbus TCP/IP communication can be configured, specifying an IP Address (192.168.3.6), Service Port (502), and Alias (127.0.0.1).
The connection settings also include serial communication parameters such as Baud Rate (19200), Word Length (8 bits), Parity (NONE), and Stop Bits (1).
Hardware Flow Control options, including DTR and RTS control, are present but shown as disabled with zero delay.
The document instructs the user to configure connection details as presented in Figure 2 and then click OK.

chunks.page_summary: This page details how to configure Modbus TCP input registers for Rheonics viscosity and density sensors using ModScan software, focusing on Float32 and Slnt16 register formats and providing examples for common parameters.
chunks.extracted_text:
Next, select the Input Registers of interest. Modbus TCP - Input Registers: Rheonics Support (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/8100297664-modbus-tcp-input-registers) shows a complete list of Input Registers available for Rheonics Viscosity and Density sensors. The most used are listed on Table 1.

Table 1. Most used Input Registers - Float32
Parameter Name | Address (Dec) | Register (Dec) | Length (Reg) | Type | Description
Temperature Median | 56 | 300057 | 2 | Float32 | Parameter 2 value as float
Viscosity Last Good | 136 | 300137 | 2 | Float32 | Parameter 12 value as float
Density Last Good | 144 | 300145 | 2 | Float32 | Parameter 13 value as float

In this example, a single Modbus request with the maximum allowable length (125) is used to view the majority of the records at once. However, the length can be adjusted to include only the specific records required for the user's application.

Fill the options as shown on Figure 3.

[ModScan64 - ModScan641 (127.0.0.1) application window content]
File Connection Setup View Window Help
ModScan641 (127.0.0.1)
Address: 0041
Length: 125
Device Id: 255
MODBUS Point Type: 04: INPUT REGISTER
Number of Polls: 10
Valid Slave Responses: 9
Reset Ctrs

Figure 3. ModScan Input Register Settings

Rheonics Input Registers come in two formats: Float32 and Slnt16 as detailed in Modbus TCP - Input Registers (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/8100297664-modbus-tcp-input-registers). To read Float32 Registers, select Float (MSRF), as shown on Figure 4.

[ModScan64 - ModScan641 (127.0.0.1) application window content]
File Connection Setup View Window Help
ModScan641 (127.0.0.1)
Float(MSRF) option highlighted

Figure 4. ModScan - Float (MSRF)
key_facts: A complete list of Input Registers for Rheonics Viscosity and Density sensors is available on the Rheonics support website.
The most used Input Registers for Rheonics sensors include Temperature Median, Viscosity Last Good, and Density Last Good, all in Float32 format.
A single Modbus request can have a maximum allowable length of 125 records.
The length of a Modbus request can be adjusted to include only specific records required for the user's application.
Rheonics Input Registers are available in two formats: Float32 and Slnt16.
To read Float32 Registers using ModScan, the 'Float (MSRF)' option must be selected.
diagram_labels: Table 1. Most used Input Registers - Float32

3. References

1. Rheonics Support, "Modbus TCP - Input Registers," Feb. 29, 2024. [Online]. Available: Modbus Input Registers (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297459). [Accessed: Jan. 6, 2025].

2. Rheonics Support, "Using Modbuspoll pre-configured files for Rheonics Modbus Devices," Rheonics Support Portal, Feb. 29, 2024. [Online]. Available: Using Modbuspoll pre-configured files for Rheonics Modbus Devices (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000397127). [Accessed: Jan. 6, 2025].
key_facts: Figure 5 illustrates the Input Registers for a Rheonics Viscosity Sensor, with specific parameters from Table 1 highlighted in red.
The ModScan application is used to display Float32 Input Registers.
Rheonics provides support articles detailing Modbus TCP - Input Registers and using Modbuspoll pre-configured files for Rheonics Modbus Devices.
The referenced support articles were published on February 29, 2024, and accessed on January 6, 2025.
